domain:
In a e-commerce retail organization, customers are registered with unique ids to place orders that includes multiple products. An order also has a unique identifier and consists of one or more products, which are described by their name and description. When the customer completes the order, it is necessary to specify the quantity requested and the price for the item is updated.

The retailer also ask customers to provide feedback by writing reviews for specific products. These reviews capture the opinion of a customer regarding a product, including the date it was written and a rating score. Finally, the marketing department organizes promotional campaigns. A product may be included in various promotions over time. Whenever a product is part of a campaign, specific terms are established for that offer, such as the applicable discount rate and a validity period defined by a start and end date.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of OrderItem in the relationship with Product?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a product can exist without any associated order item.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a product must have at least one associated order item.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Product can exist without OrderItem.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Product must have at least one OrderItem.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Product must have at least one OrderItem.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of OrderItem in the relationship with Product?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a product is associated with exactly one order item.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a product can be associated with multiple order items.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each product links to one order item.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each product can link to multiple order items.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each product links to one order item.

questions.question:
Should OrderItem be modeled as an association class linking Order and Product, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Regular class: OrderItem is a class that has links to Order and Product. In this case multiple order items can exist for every Order and Product.
2. Association class: OrderItem exists only as part of the relationship between Order and Product. A order item cannot exist without the link between Order and Product.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Order and Product.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, OrderItem as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Order and Product.

questions.question:
Should Review be modeled as an association class linking Customer and Product, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Regular class: Review is a class that has links to Customer and Product. In this case multiple reviews can exist for every Customer and Product.
2. Association class: Review exists only as part of the relationship between Customer and Product. A review cannot exist without the link between Customer and Product.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Review as association class linking Customer and Product.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Review as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Review as association class linking Customer and Product.

questions.question:
Should ProductPromotion be modeled as an association class linking Product and Promotion, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Regular class: ProductPromotion is a class that has links to Product and Promotion. In this case multiple product promotions can exist for every Product and Promotion.
2. Association class: ProductPromotion exists only as part of the relationship between Product and Promotion. A product promotion cannot exist without the link between Product and Promotion.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, ProductPromotion as association class linking Product and Promotion.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, ProductPromotion as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, ProductPromotion as association class linking Product and Promotion.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: Association class, ProductPromotion as association class linking Product and Promotion.  

This option is better because the domain description implies that a product promotion is inherently tied to both a product and a promotion, suggesting that it cannot exist independently of this relationship.
